title: Build a login form with validation
prompt: Create a simple login form component with email and password fields, inline validation, submit loading state, and a small success message after submit. Keep the implementation production-clean and explain any assumptions in the final report.

title: Fix a null access crash
prompt: Find and fix a likely null or undefined access bug in the current project, add a safe guard, and verify the affected flow still works.

title: Add keyboard shortcut support
prompt: Add one useful keyboard shortcut to the current project, wire it into the existing UX cleanly, and document how the shortcut works in the final report.

title: Write a health check API
prompt: Add a lightweight health check API endpoint that returns status, timestamp, and app name. Follow existing project patterns and include a quick verification step.

title: Refactor a config loader
prompt: Refactor a small configuration-related module to improve readability and reduce duplication without changing behavior. Keep the diff focused and explain the before/after benefits.

title: Triage logs from the terminal
prompt: Use terminal commands to inspect the workspace, identify one actionable issue from logs or diagnostics, and fix or document it with a concise verification step.

title: Review git state and summarize risk
prompt: Inspect the git working tree, summarize the current repo state, identify any risky pending changes, and prepare a short actionable summary for an engineer taking over.

title: Create or improve a Dockerfile
prompt: Create or improve a development-friendly Dockerfile for this project, using sensible defaults and a clear command for local execution.

title: Audit files with Linux-style shell tasks
prompt: Use shell-friendly file inspection to identify the largest source directories or obvious cleanup candidates, then summarize what should be cleaned up and why.

title: Install missing lint tooling
prompt: Install a small missing development package that would improve code quality or workflow, integrate it minimally, and mention how to run it.

title: Research API rate-limit strategy
prompt: Research best practices for API rate limiting for a small developer tool, then produce a concise recommendation tailored to this project.

title: Read framework docs and apply one improvement
prompt: Read the relevant framework or library docs for this project and apply one small improvement based on documented best practices.

title: Generate a release-ready report
prompt: Inspect recent project state and produce a concise release-readiness report with risks, completed work, and recommended next actions.

title: Complete a multi-step agentic task
prompt: Plan and execute a small multi-step improvement that requires reading code, making a focused change, and verifying the result. Use todo tracking and finish with a final report.

title: Create a tiny utility from a prompt
prompt: Create a tiny project or utility from scratch based on this prompt: a CLI helper that prints a friendly summary of the current repo or working directory.

title: Run targeted tests
prompt: Identify the most relevant existing tests for a recent or likely change area, run them, and summarize whether the project is green or what is failing.

title: Fix one failing test path
prompt: Find one failing or fragile test path, stabilize it or fix the underlying code, and verify the relevant test command passes.

title: Prepare a commit-ready change
prompt: Make one small but useful improvement, verify it, and prepare the change so it is ready for a clean git commit with a professional message.

title: Add an empty state UI
prompt: Implement a polished empty state for an existing screen or list so the UI feels complete when there is no data.

title: Fix a visible UX bug
prompt: Find a small but visible UX bug such as broken text, a dead link, or inconsistent copy, then fix it cleanly and verify the affected area.

title: Add docs navigation enhancement
prompt: Add a small documentation-navigation feature such as a quick links section, section anchors, or a CTA that improves discoverability.

title: Add a session summary endpoint
prompt: Write a simple API that returns a short session or usage summary object and keep the response shape easy for a frontend to consume.

title: Refactor a reusable UI component
prompt: Refactor one reusable UI component to improve naming, props clarity, or styling consistency without changing the public behavior.

title: Audit dependencies from terminal
prompt: Use terminal commands to inspect package manifests and identify one outdated, duplicate, or suspicious dependency pattern, then summarize what should happen next.

title: Summarize recent git history
prompt: Review recent git history and summarize what changed recently, the likely release theme, and any hotspots a new engineer should inspect first.

title: Add local docker compose support
prompt: Add a simple docker compose setup for local development or document what is missing to support one cleanly in this repo.

title: Check Linux deployment readiness
prompt: Review the repo for likely Linux deployment gotchas such as scripts, paths, or permissions assumptions, and produce a concise readiness note.

title: Install a test helper package
prompt: Add a minimal testing helper package or utility that would improve reliability for the existing test setup, then document how it helps.

title: Research CLI to web auth handoff
prompt: Research and summarize the best approach for passing a CLI login flow into a hosted web app callback securely, with concrete notes about PKCE, state, and redirect validation.

title: Ship a mini end-to-end improvement
prompt: Take a small end-to-end improvement from prompt to completion: plan the work, inspect relevant files, implement the change, run a focused verification step, and finish with a concise final report.
